title: DEVOPS: pact-y30 (post-apathy revision)
description: DEVOPS wave for pact-y30 post-apathy audit. Revises stale pact-q6y artifacts to align with apathy principle. Decisions: local deployment (no cloud), no containers, GitHub Actions (existing CI), structured stderr logging, npm publish (recreate), trunk-based development.
close_reason: DEVOPS complete (post-apathy revision). Revised all 5 deliver/ artifacts: platform-architecture, ci-cd-pipeline, observability-design, monitoring-alerting, branching-strategy. Supersedes stale pact-q6y. Key decisions: zero infrastructure changes, extend existing ci.yml (3 stages: check/security/mutation), structured stderr logging, trunk-based development, 9-PR sequence, npm publish via tag-triggered workflow. Peer review: APPROVED (9/10, platform-architect-reviewer). Apathy-clean: zero references to cut concepts. Ready for DISTILL.

title: DISCOVER: Pact inheritance (layered defaults)
description: Validate whether pact inheritance/layered defaults solve a real problem at target scale (~100 users, 20-30 repos). Analyze 6-8 real-world inheritance systems (Helm, CSS, Docker Compose, Terraform, OpenAPI, GitHub Actions, Protobuf, YAML anchors). Determine resolution semantics, token cost, and complexity cliff. Evidence-first: start from duplication pain, not solution. Brief: docs/discovery/pact-inherit/brief.md. Origin: DISCUSS wave Q11 (pact-fmt).
close_reason: Folded into pact-format-spec.md. Inheritance is a catalog/presentation concern — single-level, shallow merge, colon naming. No separate implementation needed.

comments.author: Cory Shepherdson
comments.text: Surfaced during DISCUSS wave for pact-fmt. Cory described a helm-chart-like layering model: protocol_defaults → base_pact → child_pact. Key insight: teams shouldn't duplicate pact files to make domain variations. The 8 default pacts (pact-def) become the base layer that teams inherit from. Blocked on pact-fmt completing the defaults: section design first. Revisit criteria: when teams start duplicating pact definitions.

title: Implement compressed catalog format for pact_discover
description:
Implement the two-representation model from the format spec:
- Catalog entry: minimal token representation per pact (name, description, scope, required fields summary)
- pact_discover returns catalog entries by default (not full metadata)
- Add optional `detail: true` parameter to pact_discover for full metadata retrieval
- Target: ~15-25 tokens per catalog entry (94% reduction from full PACT.md loading)
- Consider pipe-delimited or structured compact format for catalog output
close_reason: Delivered in pact-dsc DELIVER wave. Compressed pipe-delimited catalog (format: compressed) returns name|description|scope|context→response per line. Deviation: param is format: full|compressed (not detail: true). Scope filtering also implemented.

title: Create 8 default global pacts in flat-file format
description:
Write the 8 default pacts as flat .md files with YAML frontmatter per docs/discovery/pact-format-spec.md:

1. ask.md — get input that unblocks current work
2. propose.md — workshop an idea through structured iteration (multi_round: true)
3. share.md — push context, no action required
4. request.md — ask someone to do something specific and deliver a result
5. handoff.md — transfer ownership of in-progress work with full context
6. check-in.md — async status round across a group
7. decide.md — collective decision with structured options
8. review.md — structured feedback with blocking/advisory split (multi_round: true)

Each pact's frontmatter is agent guidance — it tells agents what behavior to exhibit (response_mode, visibility, claimable, etc). The protocol presents this in the catalog; agents interpret and follow it. Nothing in these definitions implies protocol enforcement.

Each pact must:
- Have frontmatter sufficient for an agent to compose a valid request without reading the body
- Include one worked example in the body (request + response pair)
- Follow token efficiency guidelines (terse descriptions, no redundancy)
- Pass the litmus test: agent does meaningful work on both sides, context too rich for a text
close_reason: Created 8 default global pacts in pact-store/: ask, propose, share, request, handoff, check-in, decide, review. All YAML frontmatter with scope:global, worked examples in body, token-efficient. 335/335 tests pass including flat-file loader tests.

title: DELIVER: pact-y30 (flat-file format, catalog metadata, group addressing)
close_reason: DELIVER wave complete. 11 roadmap steps, 5-phase TDD, L1-L4 refactoring, adversarial review (1 revision), mutation testing 80.55% (915/1136). 335 tests, 17 commits. Evolution doc: docs/evolution/2026-02-24-pact-fmt.md. Remaining epic work: pact-def (8 default pacts) and pact-ret (retire old examples).

title: Feature: pact-protocol-evolution
description: PACT protocol refactoring — Transport SPI extraction, attachment externalization, and scalable git patterns.
close_reason: All children closed. Protocol evolution complete: flat-file pact store, catalog metadata, group addressing, apathy principle fully embedded.

title: Finalize pact format spec with group envelope primitives
description:
Update docs/discovery/pact-format-spec.md to incorporate:
- `defaults` section in frontmatter for envelope behavior (response_mode, visibility, deadline_required, claimable)
- Group addressing primitives (recipients list, group ref, claimable)
- Response collection modes (all, any, quorum, none_required)
- Response visibility modes (private, shared, sequential, private_then_shared)
- Compressed catalog entry format (pipe-delimited index)
- Remove conversation-scoped pacts from scope enum (covered by broad defaults)
- Tighten the litmus test into the spec: agent does meaningful work on both sides, context too rich for a text, both sides doing creative/intellectual work
close_reason: Format spec complete: apathy principle, bundles-as-instructions, inheritance (folded from pact-bmk), 8 default pacts, canonical YAML frontmatter schema. All catalog/presentation design decided.

comments.author: Cory Shepherdson
comments.text: DISCOVER complete for group envelope primitives. Artifacts at docs/discovery/pact-fmt/. Peer review: CONDITIONAL-GO → all critical issues resolved. Key decisions: 4 response_modes (any/all/quorum/none_required), 2 visibility modes (shared/private), claimable boolean, defaults section. Deferred: sequential, private_then_shared, multi-group, watchers. Ready for DISCUSS wave.

title: Migrate pact-loader from directory-per-pact to flat-file glob
description:
Change pact-loader.ts path resolution:
- From: `pacts/{name}/PACT.md` (single directory per pact)
- To: `{store_root}/**/*.md` (recursive glob, flat files)
- Pact name = filename stem (e.g. `ask.md` → `ask`)
- Add PACT_STORE config for store root path
- Drop schema.json fallback (readSchemaIfValid and schemaBundleToBundleSpec)
- Drop Markdown table fallback (parseMarkdownTables) — all pacts must use YAML frontmatter
- Update pact-discover.ts to use new glob-based discovery
- Update pact-request.ts pact existence check
close_reason: Delivered in pact-dsc DELIVER wave. Flat-file glob loader implemented in pact-loader.ts (loadFlatFilePacts, collectMdFiles). Deviations from bead: kept old pacts/ fallback for backward compat (not dropped), no PACT_STORE config (hardcoded pact-store/), pact name from YAML name field (not filename stem). 335 tests, 80.55% mutation kill rate.

title: Add recipients[] and per-respondent response storage to RequestEnvelope
description:
Add multi-recipient support to RequestEnvelope schema in src/schemas.ts:
- Replace `recipient: UserRef` with `recipients: UserRef[]` (array, always — single recipient = [user])
- Add optional `group_ref: string` (pass-through label, e.g. "@backend-team")
- Update pact-request.ts to accept `recipients: string[]` and validate all against config
- Update pact-respond.ts recipient check: `recipients.some(r => r.user_id === ctx.userId)`
- Update pact-inbox.ts filter: same array check
- Update commit message format for multi-recipient
- Per-respondent response files: `responses/{request_id}/{user_id}.json` to avoid git conflicts

NOT in scope (agent concerns, not protocol):
- No claimable, response_mode, visibility, or quorum fields on the envelope
- No defaults_applied stamping
- No claim action
- No response completion logic (auto-move based on count)
- No visibility filtering at read time

The protocol delivers to recipients and stores responses. Agents handle coordination.
close_reason: Delivered in pact-dsc DELIVER wave. recipients[] on RequestEnvelope, group_ref, per-respondent storage (responses/{id}/{user}.json), inbox enrichment (recipients_count, group_ref). Deviation: kept singular recipient field alongside recipients[] for backward compat.

title: DESIGN: pact-fmt
close_reason: DESIGN complete. Architecture: modular monolith (no change). ADRs: 017 (per-respondent storage), 018 (defaults merge), 019 (exclusive claim). Peer review: APPROVED. Ready for DEVOPS.

title: Extend PactMetadata with catalog fields (scope, defaults, extends, attachments)
description:
Extend PactMetadata interface in src/pact-loader.ts with fields from the format spec:
- Add `scope: 'global' | 'org' | 'repo' | 'team'`
- Add `registered_for: string[]`
- Add `multi_round: boolean`
- Add `extends: string` (parent pact name for inheritance resolution)
- Add `defaults: { response_mode?, visibility?, claimable? }` — parsed from frontmatter, presented in catalog as agent guidance (NOT merged or enforced at runtime by the protocol)
- Add `attachments: AttachmentSlot[]` (slot name, required, convention, description)
- Update YAML frontmatter parsing to extract these fields
- Update PactCatalogEntry in pact-discover.ts to include new fields in discovery output
- Resolve inheritance at load time: if `extends` present, load parent, shallow-merge per spec rules, return resolved result. Agents see a flat catalog.

All of these are catalog/presentation concerns. The protocol parses and presents them; agents interpret and act on them.

NOT in scope:
- No defaults-merge.ts function
- No defaults_applied stamping on request envelopes
- No runtime enforcement of any metadata field
close_reason: Delivered in pact-dsc DELIVER wave. PactMetadata extended with scope, registered_for, multi_round, extends, defaults, attachments. Inheritance resolution at load time (resolveInheritance, mergeChildOverParent). Deviation: defaults is Record<string, unknown> (opaque pass-through) not typed PactDefaults — aligns with apathy principle.

title: DISCOVER: pact-protocol-evolution
description: Evidence-based discovery to validate assumptions in the refactoring plan: Transport SPI need, git scaling patterns, smallest valuable slice.
close_reason: Discovery complete — 5 artifacts at docs/discovery/. Key finding: Gerrit model premature, layered scaling recommended.

title: DEVOPS: pact-fmt
description: Platform readiness and infrastructure design for pact-fmt (group envelope primitives). CI/CD pipeline extensions, observability alignment, branching strategy, and deployment design.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Cory Shepherdson
comments.text: DEVOPS wave complete for pact-fmt. 5 artifacts delivered to docs/feature/pact-fmt/deliver/: platform-architecture.md, ci-cd-pipeline.md, observability-design.md, monitoring-alerting.md, branching-strategy.md. Peer review: CONDITIONALLY APPROVED → 1 critical (git grep syntax) fixed. Key decisions: extend existing ci.yml with security+mutation stages, structured JSON logging to stderr (zero-cost), GitHub Flow branching, 7-PR sequence by user story. Ready for DISTILL wave.

title: Retire old example pacts and schema.json files
description:
Delete the old directory-per-pact format entirely:
- examples/pacts/ask/ (PACT.md + schema.json)
- examples/pacts/code-review/ (PACT.md + schema.json)
- examples/pacts/sanity-check/ (PACT.md + schema.json)
- examples/pacts/design-pact/ (PACT.md + schema.json)

The 8 new default pacts (pact-def) replace these. The old examples served their purpose during development; they don't need to be preserved or migrated. code-review → review variant, sanity-check → ask variant, design-pact → propose. Teams will create their own variants from the new base pacts.
close_reason: Retired examples/pacts/ directory-per-pact format. All code and 335 tests now use pact-store/ flat-file YAML frontmatter. Added loadFlatFilePactByName(), updated inbox enrichment, pact-init.sh, and 13 test files.

title: DISCOVER: Pact template/schema formalization audit
description:
Audit existing pact template/schema/example files (pacts/ask/, pacts/code-review/, pacts/sanity-check/) and formalize them into the new flat-file pact store format decided in the DISCOVER wave.

Context:
- Design decided: single pact store per org, flat {name}.md files, YAML frontmatter for all metadata
- Current pacts use directory-per-pact convention (pacts/{name}/PACT.md) — needs migration
- schema.json fallback to be dropped — all metadata in frontmatter
- Need to define the complete frontmatter schema (scope, registered_for, hooks, context_bundle, response_bundle)
- Built-in defaults (ask, sanity-check, code-review) become scope: global
- Ensure the template format serves the 100-user org deployment target

See: docs/discovery/problem-validation.md (Problem 3b) and docs/discovery/opportunity-tree.md (OE10)
notes: Format spec written at docs/discovery/pact-format-spec.md. Covers: canonical YAML frontmatter schema, token efficiency design (Vercel AGENTS.md research applied), two-representation model (compressed catalog + full retrieval), scope patterns, migration path from directory-per-pact, and open questions. Ready for review/iteration.
close_reason: Discovery complete. Format spec at docs/discovery/pact-format-spec.md. Default pact set defined (8 pacts). Group addressing primitives identified. Ready for implementation.

title: Update tests for flat-file loader, catalog metadata, and recipients[]
description:
Update the test suite to cover the trimmed pact-y30 scope:

Loader & catalog:
- pact-loader: YAML frontmatter parsing for new fields (scope, registered_for, defaults, multi_round, extends, attachments)
- pact-loader: inheritance resolution (extends → shallow merge → flat result)
- pact-loader: flat-file glob resolution (`{store_root}/**/*.md`)
- pact-discover: catalog format output with new metadata fields
- pact-discover: scope filtering

Transport:
- schemas: RequestEnvelope with `recipients: UserRef[]` replacing `recipient: UserRef`
- schemas: optional `group_ref: string`
- pact-request: multi-recipient send (validate all recipients against config)
- pact-respond: per-respondent response files (`responses/{id}/{user}.json`)
- pact-respond: recipient check against array
- pact-inbox: filter by `recipients.some()`

NOT in scope (removed from protocol):
- No claim action tests
- No response completion logic tests (response_mode counting)
- No visibility filtering tests
- No defaults-merge function tests
- No defaults_applied envelope tests
close_reason: Delivered in pact-dsc DELIVER wave. 4 acceptance test files (y30-flat-file-loader, y30-compressed-catalog, y30-group-walking-skeleton, y30-group-error-paths) + mutation-hardening.test.ts. 216 new tests (119→335 total). 80.55% mutation kill rate across 1136 mutants.

title: Pact store: flat-file format, catalog metadata, and default pacts
description:
Migrate the pact store to flat-file format with rich catalog metadata for agents.

Catalog/presentation:
- Flat-file loader: `{store_root}/**/*.md` replacing `pacts/{name}/PACT.md`
- Extended PactMetadata: scope, registered_for, defaults (agent guidance), extends (inheritance), attachments
- Inheritance resolution at load time (shallow merge, single-level, flat catalog output)
- Compressed catalog format for token efficiency (~15-25 tokens per entry)
- 8 default global pacts replacing the 4 old examples

Transport (minimal):
- `recipient` → `recipients[]` on RequestEnvelope
- Optional `group_ref` metadata field
- Per-respondent response files (`responses/{id}/{user}.json`)

NOT in scope — agent concerns, not protocol:
- No claiming action or enforcement
- No response_mode completion logic
- No visibility filtering
- No defaults-merge runtime function
- No behavioral state machines

Evidence: docs/discovery/pact-format-spec.md (apathy principle, bundles-as-instructions), CLAUDE.md (first principles).
close_reason: All 15 child beads delivered. Flat-file pact store, catalog metadata, group addressing, 8 default pacts, 335 tests, 80.55% mutation kill rate. Doc cleanup completed.

Blockers resolved: migration strategies added for schema, pact store, and response layout. Ready for DEVOPS.

title: DISTILL: pact-y30 (acceptance test design)
description: Design E2E acceptance tests for pact-y30 feature set: flat-file pact store, extended metadata, group addressing (recipients[]), per-respondent responses, pact inheritance, and compressed catalog. Tests use Vitest + GWT helpers against real local git repos.
close_reason: DISTILL complete. 29 acceptance scenarios (4 files, 55% error coverage, 3 walking skeletons). Vitest + GWT, all @skip pending impl. Artifacts: tests/acceptance/y30-*.test.ts, docs/feature/pact-fmt/distill/
